title: Air-fryer onion rings
subtitle: Crunchy panko-coated onion rings without deep-frying
excerpt: Thick onion rings dipped in egg, coated in seasoned panko breadcrumbs, and cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 10 to 12 minutes. The panko coating gives a crispier result than a batter in the air fryer because it browns directly in the circulating heat, whereas a wet batter tends to steam.
sourceNotes: Onion rings are an American invention that became a standard British pub and fast-food side dish. The battered version is traditional in the US and UK; the panko-breaded version is a Japanese-influenced adaptation that has become common in home cooking because it works better in an air fryer than a wet batter. Panko stays crisp after cooking; batter tends to soften as steam from the onion escapes.

content.text: Use thick-cut rings from a large onion, not thin slices. Thin onion rings burn at their edges before the panko browns. Rings cut 1 cm thick have enough onion inside to steam through at the same rate as the coating browns, producing an even result throughout.

items.name: Onion
items.prepNote: large, peeled and cut into 1 cm rings

content.text: Separate the {{onion}} slices into individual rings. Set up three shallow bowls: seasoned {{plain-flour}}, beaten {{eggs}}, and {{breadcrumbs-panko}} mixed with {{smoked-paprika}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Coat each ring in flour (shaking off the excess), dip in egg, then press firmly into the panko on both sides. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 2 minutes. Spray the basket with oil. Arrange the rings in a single layer. Spray the tops generously with oil. Cook at 200°C for 10 to 12 minutes, turning once at the 6-minute mark and spraying the second side. They are done when deep golden and the onion is completely tender inside. Serve immediately.

items.fix: Panko must be fully coated with oil to brown. Spray both sides generously. Rings must be in a single layer with no overlapping; overlapping rings steam rather than crisp
items.cause: Insufficient oil spray, or basket overcrowded
items.symptom: Coating pale and not crisping despite correct time

items.fix: Onions with thick, tightly packed layers hold together better than very large onions with loose rings. Coat each ring individually rather than as a stack
items.cause: Very large rings with many loose inner layers
items.symptom: Inner onion layers sliding out of the coating during cooking

items.fix: Keep rings to 1 cm. Raw onion at 1 cm cooks through in 12 minutes. If using a particularly pungent onion, soaking the rings in cold water for 10 minutes before coating reduces the sharpness
items.cause: Rings too thick, or onion not sweetened by the heat
items.symptom: Onion still raw and biting after 12 minutes

content.text: Air-fryer onion rings are a side dish or a sharing snack. Serve alongside burgers and chips, with a smoky mayonnaise dip, or stacked on a burger as a topping. They soften as they cool, so serve immediately after cooking.
